message:
fix(fr-command): zset error wording matches upstream [bd-frankenredis-uczv]

Three small zset error wording divergences from upstream Redis 7.2:

- ZRANGEBYSCORE / ZRANGE BYSCORE / ZCOUNT / ZRANGESTORE BYSCORE
  with malformed score bounds: previously yielded "ERR value is
  not a valid float" via the generic ValueNotFloat mapping;
  upstream t_zset.c::zslParseRange emits "ERR min or max is not a
  float". Switched parse_score_bound to a Custom error with the
  upstream wording so all callers benefit.

- ZINTERCARD ... LIMIT BAD or LIMIT -1: previously yielded
  "ERR value is not an integer or out of range"; upstream
  t_zset.c::zinterCardCommand emits "ERR LIMIT can't be
  negative" for both unparseable and negative inputs.

- ZUNIONSTORE/ZINTERSTORE/ZDIFFSTORE WEIGHTS BAD: previously
  yielded "ERR value is not an integer or out of range";
  upstream emits "ERR weight value is not a float" via the
  zunionInterDiffGenericCommand parser. Routed both the parse
  failure and the NaN check to that wording.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
